question: What conditions require that dry bulk tanks receive special care during transportation?

answers.text: Partially loaded dry bulk tanks can have their load shift within the tank.
answers.explanation: Several types of loads are listed in the handbook as requiring special care. Dry bulk tanks have a naturally high center of gravity, and their load may shift, especially around corners. Be sure to slow during curves to prevent rollovers.

answers.text: Dry bulk tanks have a naturally low center of gravity
Dry bulk tanks are highly susceptible to forward and back shifting.

explanation: Several types of loads are listed in the handbook as requiring special care. Dry bulk tanks have a naturally high center of gravity, and their load may shift, especially around corners. Be sure to slow during curves to prevent rollovers.

question: High beams should be:

answers.text: Turned on when an oncoming driver does not dim his/her lights.
Dimmed at the time when you get within 100 feet of another vehicle.
Used when it is safe and legal to do so.
answers.explanation: Use high beams whenever possible and allowed. Switch to low beams when the situation requires, such as when behind another vehicle, driving in a city, or a vehicle approaching in the other lane.

explanation: Use high beams whenever possible and allowed. Switch to low beams when the situation requires, such as when behind another vehicle, driving in a city, or a vehicle approaching in the other lane.

question: Which of these statements about cold weather driving is true?

answers.text: Exhaust system leaks are less dangerous in cold weather.
Windshield washer antifreeze should be used.
answers.explanation: Remember to keep the car's cooling system full, with enough antifreeze to protect against freezing.

answers.text: An engine cannot overheat when the weather is very cold.

explanation: Remember to keep the car's cooling system full, with enough antifreeze to protect against freezing.

question: Hazardous materials can be identified by:

answers.text: the packaging labels.
answers.explanation: Hazardous materials can be identified by the packaging label, the shipping paperwork, and the placards displayed on the tractor-trailer.

answers.text: the shape of the cargo.
the color scheme of the cargo.

explanation: Hazardous materials can be identified by the packaging label, the shipping paperwork, and the placards displayed on the tractor-trailer.

question: Which of these is the proper signal to change lane?

answers.text: Signal before the change and move over quickly.
Signal after you begin change and cross over slowly.
Signal early and change lane slowly and smoothly.
answers.explanation: Turn signals only work to notify other drivers when they are applied before you begin to change directions. Signal your intention to change lanes early and then slowly merge into the other lane.

explanation: Turn signals only work to notify other drivers when they are applied before you begin to change directions. Signal your intention to change lanes early and then slowly merge into the other lane.

question: Which of these is correct about emergency or evasive action?

answers.text: In order to turn quickly you must have a firm grip on the steering wheel.
answers.explanation: Emergency or evasive maneuvers require that the driver has two hands on the steering wheel to have the best control of the vehicle during the action. Drive with both hands on the wheel so that you are ready at any time.

answers.text: Stopping is always the safest thing to do in a traffic emergency.
You can stop more quickly than you can turn to miss obstacles.

explanation: Emergency or evasive maneuvers require that the driver has two hands on the steering wheel to have the best control of the vehicle during the action. Drive with both hands on the wheel so that you are ready at any time.

question: When you are starting to move up a hill from a stop:

answers.text: Keep the trailer brake hand valve applied until you reach 20 mph.
Engage the clutch and accelerate quickly.
Release the parking brakes as you apply engine power.
answers.explanation: Use the parking brake to hold the vehicle while you engage the transmission and then let out the clutch to apply engine power. Then release the parking brake.

explanation: Use the parking brake to hold the vehicle while you engage the transmission and then let out the clutch to apply engine power. Then release the parking brake.

question: Which of the following vehicles will have the longest stopping distance?

answers.text: Loaded truck.
Empty truck.
answers.explanation: The brakes of a tractor-trailer are designed to optimally stop a fully loaded vehicle. When the trailer is empty, the brakes are prone to locking up unless they are applied gently and over a longer stopping distance.

answers.text: Bobtail tractor.

explanation: The brakes of a tractor-trailer are designed to optimally stop a fully loaded vehicle. When the trailer is empty, the brakes are prone to locking up unless they are applied gently and over a longer stopping distance.

question: What are three types of vehicle inspections that a driver needs to perform?

answers.text: pre-trip, en-route, and post-trip.
answers.explanation: A pre-trip inspection is done first before heading out. En-route inspection is performed anytime you stop. The post-trip inspection is done last, once the trip is complete.

answers.text: Seven-step, five-step, and three-step.
Pre-trip, en-route, and repair verification.

explanation: A pre-trip inspection is done first before heading out. En-route inspection is performed anytime you stop. The post-trip inspection is done last, once the trip is complete.

question: To avoid a crash, you had to drive into the right shoulder. You are now driving at 40 mph on the shoulder. How should you move back into the pavement?

answers.text: If the shoulder is clear, stay on the right until your vehicle comes to a stop then move back into the pavement when it is safe.
answers.explanation: Assuming that the shoulder is clear and presents no imminent danger, slow to a stop on the shoulder. You can then start moving again and merge back onto the roadway at a more controlled speed.

answers.text: Steer sharply onto the pavement, then brake hard as you counter steer.
Brake hard to slow the vehicle, then steer sharply onto the pavement.

explanation: Assuming that the shoulder is clear and presents no imminent danger, slow to a stop on the shoulder. You can then start moving again and merge back onto the roadway at a more controlled speed.

question: Which statement is true regarding the Global Positioning System?

answers.text: Program your GPS device once you are on the road.
Select a GPS device and software that is intended for CMV navigation.
answers.explanation: Ensure that you select a device and corresponding software that is intended for CMV navigation. Your device should be updated and programmed prior to starting the trip so you do not become distracted by programming it while driving.

answers.text: Using the GPS on your smartphone will be more effective than a vehicle navigation device.

explanation: Ensure that you select a device and corresponding software that is intended for CMV navigation. Your device should be updated and programmed prior to starting the trip so you do not become distracted by programming it while driving.

question: Which strategy is recommended when you experience a tailgater following you?

answers.text: Slow slightly to increase your following distance.
answers.explanation: Do not apply your brakes or accelerate to discourage a tailgater. Maintain a safe speed of travel. You may also increase your own following distance to give the tailgater room to pull in once they pass.

answers.text: Apply your brakes firmly to panic the tailgater.
Accelerate to increase the distance between you and the tailgater.

explanation: Do not apply your brakes or accelerate to discourage a tailgater. Maintain a safe speed of travel. You may also increase your own following distance to give the tailgater room to pull in once they pass.

question: Regulations require that the loading limit of a cargo tie-down be _____ times stronger than the cargo itself.

answers.explanation: A cargo tie-down has a maximum working limit that tells you how much of a load it can secure. Federal regulations dictate that the cargo tie-down be at least one-half times the weight of the cargo. So, a 1,000-pound cargo would require tie-downs rated for 1,500 pounds.

explanation: A cargo tie-down has a maximum working limit that tells you how much of a load it can secure. Federal regulations dictate that the cargo tie-down be at least one-half times the weight of the cargo. So, a 1,000-pound cargo would require tie-downs rated for 1,500 pounds.

question: The driver's manual suggests several things to do when you pass a vehicle. Which of these is NOT one of them?

answers.text: At night, turn on your high beams before you start to pass and leave them on until you have completely passed the vehicle.
answers.explanation: The safest method to pass a vehicle, cyclist, or pedestrian is to assume they do not see you. Be aware of sudden movements as you approach them. Flash your headlights at night or lightly sound the horn. Make sure that your headlights are dimmed and carefully guide your vehicle past them while monitoring their position.

answers.text: Assume that another driver does not see you.
Lightly tap your horn.

explanation: The safest method to pass a vehicle, cyclist, or pedestrian is to assume they do not see you. Be aware of sudden movements as you approach them. Flash your headlights at night or lightly sound the horn. Make sure that your headlights are dimmed and carefully guide your vehicle past them while monitoring their position.

question: You should avoid any driver that you suspect to be driving under the influence. Symptoms may include:

answers.text: Maintaining a steady vehicle speed.
An inability to stay in their lane.
answers.explanation: Symptoms of a DUI include an inability to remain in their lane or maintain vehicle speed, and erratically moving.

answers.text: Driving slow in the right lane.

explanation: Symptoms of a DUI include an inability to remain in their lane or maintain vehicle speed, and erratically moving.

question: You should signal continuously while turning because:

answers.text: It is illegal to turn off your signal before completing the turn.
Most vehicle have self-canceling signals.
You need both hands on the wheel to turn safely.
answers.explanation: Safely navigating a commercial vehicle through a turn requires the driver to use both hands on the steering wheel until the turn is complete. Canceling a signal early should not be done since the action would require that only one hand be used during part of the turn.

explanation: Safely navigating a commercial vehicle through a turn requires the driver to use both hands on the steering wheel until the turn is complete. Canceling a signal early should not be done since the action would require that only one hand be used during part of the turn.

question: As the blood alcohol concentration (BAC) goes up, what happens?

answers.text: Judgment and self-control are affected.
answers.explanation: Blood Alcohol Content (BAC) increases as you consume more alcohol, affecting the part of your brain that allows you to have the self-control and good judgment necessary when operating a vehicle. Another side effect of alcohol is that it keeps drinkers from knowing they are getting drunk. So, do not drink if you are going to drive.

answers.text: The effects of alcohol decrease.
The drinker more clearly sees how alcohol is affecting him/her.

explanation: Blood Alcohol Content (BAC) increases as you consume more alcohol, affecting the part of your brain that allows you to have the self-control and good judgment necessary when operating a vehicle. Another side effect of alcohol is that it keeps drinkers from knowing they are getting drunk. So, do not drink if you are going to drive.

question: For your safety, when setting out reflective triangles you should:

answers.text: Turn on your flashers.
Keep them out of sight while you walk to the spots where you set them out.
Hold the triangles between yourself and oncoming traffic.
answers.explanation: For your safety, hold the triangle between yourself and the oncoming traffic. This helps you become more visible to others on the road.

explanation: For your safety, hold the triangle between yourself and the oncoming traffic. This helps you become more visible to others on the road.

question: The most important thing to remember emergency braking is:

answers.text: If the wheels are skidding, you cannot control your vehicle.
answers.explanation: If the tires are skidding the driver has no control and cannot steer. Both controlled braking and stab braking are techniques to ensure that the tires have traction with the road so the driver can control the vehicle.

answers.text: Disconnecting the steering axle brakes will keep your vehicle in a straight-line during emergency braking.
Never do it without downshifting first.

explanation: If the tires are skidding the driver has no control and cannot steer. Both controlled braking and stab braking are techniques to ensure that the tires have traction with the road so the driver can control the vehicle.

question: Drivers of trucks and tractor-trailers with cargo must check that the cargo is well secured within the first ____ miles of the trip.

answers.explanation: Commercial drivers are required to inspect the securement of their cargo within the first 50 miles. They must then inspect the cargo every three hours or 150 miles, whichever happens first.

explanation: Commercial drivers are required to inspect the securement of their cargo within the first 50 miles. They must then inspect the cargo every three hours or 150 miles, whichever happens first.

question: Extra care is needed to keep your vehicle centered in your lane because commercial vehicles:

answers.text: Require a lot of room to change lane.
Are often wider than other vehicles.
answers.explanation: Commercial vehicles tend to be significantly wider than passenger cars and light trucks. Thus, it is important that the driver pay attention to the location of the commercial truck and remain in the center of the lane.

answers.text: Tend to sway from time to time.

explanation: Commercial vehicles tend to be significantly wider than passenger cars and light trucks. Thus, it is important that the driver pay attention to the location of the commercial truck and remain in the center of the lane.

question: Which type of drivers will require you to be extra careful around when driving.

answers.text: US Postal Service truck operators.
Other CDL operators.
Rental truck operators.
answers.explanation: Rental truck operators will likely not be familiar with the vehicle that they are operating and can misjudge distances and clearances.

explanation: Rental truck operators will likely not be familiar with the vehicle that they are operating and can misjudge distances and clearances.

question: Which strategy is NOT recommended when passing another vehicle?

answers.text: Activating your four-way flashers.
answers.explanation: Your four-way flashers should be used when parked on the side of the road. Your horn and flashing headlights can be used to signal your presence when passing.

answers.text: Briefly sounding your horn.
Flashing your headlights quickly to high and back to low beams.

explanation: Your four-way flashers should be used when parked on the side of the road. Your horn and flashing headlights can be used to signal your presence when passing.

question: When driving at night, you should:

answers.text: Dim your lights within 300 feet of an oncoming vehicle.
Adjust your speed to keep your stopping distance within your sight distance.
answers.explanation: During night drives, your headlight makes you visible to others. However, remember that, despite the beam from your headlight, it's still difficult to see at night. Your low beam gives you about 250 feet of visibility, and your high beam lets you see 350 to 500 feet ahead. Adjust your speed to keep your stopping distance within your sight distance.

answers.text: Look to the left side of the road when a vehicle is coming towards you.

explanation: During night drives, your headlight makes you visible to others. However, remember that, despite the beam from your headlight, it's still difficult to see at night. Your low beam gives you about 250 feet of visibility, and your high beam lets you see 350 to 500 feet ahead. Adjust your speed to keep your stopping distance within your sight distance.

question: Which statement correctly correlates traction with vehicle speed?

answers.text: Cold road surfaces offer better traction than warm surfaces.
A driver should park and wait for weather conditions to improve whenever it is snowing.
Stopping distance is increased when driving under slippery road conditions.
answers.explanation: Traction is reduced during slippery road conditions resulting in increased stopping distance. The proper reaction to slippery conditions is to slow your vehicle speed. The recommendations are to reduce speed by 1/3 for wet/rainy conditions, 1/2 for snowy conditions, and to park and wait for weather conditions to improve whenever it is icy.

explanation: Traction is reduced during slippery road conditions resulting in increased stopping distance. The proper reaction to slippery conditions is to slow your vehicle speed. The recommendations are to reduce speed by 1/3 for wet/rainy conditions, 1/2 for snowy conditions, and to park and wait for weather conditions to improve whenever it is icy.

question: What happens to tar on the road pavement during hot weather?

answers.text: Nothing.
It bleeds making the road surface slippery.
answers.explanation: In hot weather the tar in the pavement can bleed to the road surface causing a slippery road condition.

answers.text: It becomes staining making tires stick to the road.

explanation: In hot weather the tar in the pavement can bleed to the road surface causing a slippery road condition.

question: How many hours may a CMV driver be on duty within a seven-day period?

answers.explanation: A CDL holder may not be on duty more than 60 hours within seven days and 70 hours within eight days.

explanation: A CDL holder may not be on duty more than 60 hours within seven days and 70 hours within eight days.

question: While stopping along a two-way, two-lane roadway, the terrain makes it difficult for approaching drivers to see the parked truck. What is the maximum distance that you can place a warning device behind the vehicle?

answers.text: 100 feet
200 feet
500 feet
answers.explanation: When the terrain, such as a sharp curve, blocks the truck from the view of approaching vehicles, you may place a warning device up to 500 feet behind the truck.

explanation: When the terrain, such as a sharp curve, blocks the truck from the view of approaching vehicles, you may place a warning device up to 500 feet behind the truck.

question: Best practices for turn signal usage include:

answers.text: Activate the signal when you start to turn.
Keep both hands on the steering wheel through the turn.
answers.explanation: First, signal early so other drivers know your intention to turn. Second, keep both hands on the steering wheel to maintain control while turning and let the signal keep flashing. Third, once the turn is complete, if the signal has not turned off automatically, you may manually turn off the signal.

answers.text: A turn signal is not necessary if you are already in a left turn lane at a traffic light.

explanation: First, signal early so other drivers know your intention to turn. Second, keep both hands on the steering wheel to maintain control while turning and let the signal keep flashing. Third, once the turn is complete, if the signal has not turned off automatically, you may manually turn off the signal.

question: Which use of vehicle lighting is NOT recommended?

answers.text: The use of headlights anytime visibility is impaired
Activating four-way flashers when you see an accident ahead and start to slow down.
Tapping the service brakes to slow and shine the brake lights to get a tailgater to stop.
answers.explanation: You are not recommended to use your brakes nor your brake lights to discourage a tailgater. Simply stay in the right lane and perhaps increase your following distance to give them room to pass.

explanation: You are not recommended to use your brakes nor your brake lights to discourage a tailgater. Simply stay in the right lane and perhaps increase your following distance to give them room to pass.

question: If you do not have a CB-radio, what procedure should you follow on an accident scene?

answers.text: Care for the injured person.
Notify the authorities.
Protect the area.
answers.explanation: In this scenario, the highest priority is to prevent additional accidents from occurring at the location that will compound the initial problem.

explanation: In this scenario, the highest priority is to prevent additional accidents from occurring at the location that will compound the initial problem.

question: When starting a bus on a level surface with good traction there is often no need for:

answers.text: Parking brakes.
answers.explanation: If the bus is on a level surface with good traction, then the parking brake is not likely needed to hold the bus when starting. Simply start the bus, engage the transmission, and begin moving.

answers.text: A tire check.
Slow acceleration.

explanation: If the bus is on a level surface with good traction, then the parking brake is not likely needed to hold the bus when starting. Simply start the bus, engage the transmission, and begin moving.

question: If you have a road emergency and must park on the shoulder, how soon should you put out your triangles?

answers.text: Within 20 minutes.
Within 15 minutes.
Within 10 minutes.
answers.explanation: If you will be parked on the side of the road for more than 10 minutes, it is important to place your reflective triangles leading up to the location of your truck.

explanation: If you will be parked on the side of the road for more than 10 minutes, it is important to place your reflective triangles leading up to the location of your truck.

question: While monitoring the roadway while driving, which of the following may cause you to adjust your speed?

answers.text: Number of lanes available
Being behind or ahead of schedule
Traffic congestion
answers.explanation: While monitoring the roadway 12 to 15 seconds ahead, you will adjust your speed for traffic congestion, weather conditions, visibility, upgrades, and downgrades.

explanation: While monitoring the roadway 12 to 15 seconds ahead, you will adjust your speed for traffic congestion, weather conditions, visibility, upgrades, and downgrades.

question: Most serious skids result from:

answers.text: An uneven load.
Turning too sharply.
Driving too fast for conditions.
answers.explanation: Traveling too fast for road conditions is the primary cause of skids. Try to avoid oversteering, over-accelerating, and over-braking.

explanation: Traveling too fast for road conditions is the primary cause of skids. Try to avoid oversteering, over-accelerating, and over-braking.

question: You are checking your tires for a pre-trip inspection. Which of these statements is true?

answers.text: Radial and bias-ply tires can be used together on the same vehicle.
Tires of mismatched sizes should not be used together in the same vehicle.
answers.explanation: It is dangerous to have tires of different sizes in the same vehicle.

answers.text: 2/32-inch tread depth is safe for front tires.

explanation: It is dangerous to have tires of different sizes in the same vehicle.

question: When should the engine oil pressure gauge begin to register pressure when the engine is started?

answers.text: Immediately
answers.explanation: Oil pressure is to an engine what a pulse is to a person. Pressure should immediately begin to register the moment an engine starts. If there is a delay or the pressure is low, have a technician inspect the engine lubrication system.

answers.text: Within 10 seconds
When the engine reaches operating temperature

explanation: Oil pressure is to an engine what a pulse is to a person. Pressure should immediately begin to register the moment an engine starts. If there is a delay or the pressure is low, have a technician inspect the engine lubrication system.

question: Which of the following statements about speed management is true?

answers.text: Empty truck and loaded trucks will have the same stopping distance.
If you double your speed, stopping distance will double.
If you double your speed, stopping distance will be increased by 4 times.
answers.explanation: An increase in speed results in an exponential increase in vehicle mass. Doubling your speed will actually require a stopping distance that is four times greater rather than double the distance.

explanation: An increase in speed results in an exponential increase in vehicle mass. Doubling your speed will actually require a stopping distance that is four times greater rather than double the distance.

question: Which fluid level is NOT included during the engine compartment inspection when conducting the seven-step inspection method?

answers.text: automatic transmission fluid.
power steering fluid.
diesel fuel.
answers.explanation: Engine compartment fluid levels to check include transmission, wipers, engine coolant, power steering, engine oil, and battery (if located in the engine compartment).

explanation: Engine compartment fluid levels to check include transmission, wipers, engine coolant, power steering, engine oil, and battery (if located in the engine compartment).

question: Regarding cargo, which of the following is NOT a responsibility of the driver?

answers.text: Ensuring the cargo is loaded correctly and secured.
Ensuring that placards are properly placed for hazardous materials shipments.
Filling out the shipping paperwork and creating the packaging labels.
answers.explanation: Drivers ensure that cargo is properly loaded, balanced, and secured. That hazardous materials have the proper placards in place with the correct labels and shipping papers. Filling out the paperwork and labels is the responsibility of the shipper.

explanation: Drivers ensure that cargo is properly loaded, balanced, and secured. That hazardous materials have the proper placards in place with the correct labels and shipping papers. Filling out the paperwork and labels is the responsibility of the shipper.

question: Should brake adjustment be checked often?

answers.text: No, because the other brakes will make up for the air brakes when they are out of adjustment.
It depends on how the brakes are.
Yes, because the brakes can get out of adjustment when they are used a lot.
answers.explanation: Brakes can get out of adjustment quickly, especially when they are used a lot; also, brake linings wear faster when they are hot. Therefore, brake adjustment must be checked frequently.

explanation: Brakes can get out of adjustment quickly, especially when they are used a lot; also, brake linings wear faster when they are hot. Therefore, brake adjustment must be checked frequently.

question: What is the best way to remain alert while driving?

answers.text: Roll down the window and turn up the radio.
Consume energy or other caffeinated drinks.
Get a full night’s rest before the trip and take breaks when needed.
answers.explanation: Avoid any medications that may cause drowsiness. Get at least 8 hours of sleep the night before a trip, and be sure to take breaks every couple of hours.

explanation: Avoid any medications that may cause drowsiness. Get at least 8 hours of sleep the night before a trip, and be sure to take breaks every couple of hours.

question: If you are convicted for driving under the influence while driving a commercial vehicle, and this is your first offense, you most likely will get:

answers.text: A probation period for 1 year.
A fine of $250.
At least one-year suspension of your CDL.
answers.explanation: Convictions for driving under the influence are harsher for commercial drivers than for a normal passenger vehicle driver. If found guilty and it is your first offense, you will likely have your CDL suspended for a year.

explanation: Convictions for driving under the influence are harsher for commercial drivers than for a normal passenger vehicle driver. If found guilty and it is your first offense, you will likely have your CDL suspended for a year.

question: You are inspecting hydraulic brakes. You should pump the brake pedal three times then apply firm pressure to the pedal for five seconds. If the brakes are working properly, the pedal should:

answers.text: Depress slightly.
Not move.
answers.explanation: Hydraulic brake fluid does not compress. Therefore, holding steady pressure on the pedal after pumping it three times should result in a pedal that stays hard. If the brake pedal begins to fade or sinks to the floor, seek out service immediately.

answers.text: Sink to the floor.

explanation: Hydraulic brake fluid does not compress. Therefore, holding steady pressure on the pedal after pumping it three times should result in a pedal that stays hard. If the brake pedal begins to fade or sinks to the floor, seek out service immediately.

question: Which of these distances related to space management must you choose to increase during slippery road conditions?

answers.text: Reaction distance
Following distance
answers.explanation: Slippery conditions will result in increased braking distance. Therefore, to allow additional time and space to stop in time, you must increase following distance.

answers.text: Braking distance

explanation: Slippery conditions will result in increased braking distance. Therefore, to allow additional time and space to stop in time, you must increase following distance.

question: If your brakes fail on a downgrade, you must first:

answers.text: Look outside your vehicle for another means of slowing you.
answers.explanation: If the brakes have failed completely you will need to seek an alternative in order to stop. Look for an escape ramp or convenient terrain to safely stop the vehicle.

answers.text: Hit some object in order to stop your vehicle.
Find an escape ramp in order to exit the roadway.

explanation: If the brakes have failed completely you will need to seek an alternative in order to stop. Look for an escape ramp or convenient terrain to safely stop the vehicle.

question: Each of these is a recommended protocol for traversing a working zone, EXCEPT:

answers.text: Activate your four-way flashers as you begin to slow down while approaching a work zone
Slow down to the work zone speed limit and be mindful of worker locations.
Once in the work zone sound your horn to warn workers of your presence.
answers.explanation: When approaching a work zone, slow down to the appropriate speed limit. Turn on your four-way hazards to communicate the situation to drivers that are behind you. Be mindful of worker locations and any potential hazards that may require an immediate response.

explanation: When approaching a work zone, slow down to the appropriate speed limit. Turn on your four-way hazards to communicate the situation to drivers that are behind you. Be mindful of worker locations and any potential hazards that may require an immediate response.

question: Which type of emergency equipment is not required, but recommended to take on a trip?

answers.text: Tire chains
answers.explanation: Optional emergency equipment includes tire chains, tire changing tools, and an emergency contact list of phone numbers.

answers.text: Reflective triangles and flares
Properly charged fire extinguisher

explanation: Optional emergency equipment includes tire chains, tire changing tools, and an emergency contact list of phone numbers.

question: When going down a long steep downgrade you should always:

answers.text: Brake when you exceed safe speed by 5 mph.
Use controlled braking.
Use the braking effect of the engine.
answers.explanation: Engine braking (or a retarder) should be the primary method of controlling your speed on a steep downgrade. The service brakes should be used to supplement the engine braking.

explanation: Engine braking (or a retarder) should be the primary method of controlling your speed on a steep downgrade. The service brakes should be used to supplement the engine braking.

question: Your vehicle is in a traffic emergency and may collide with another vehicle if you do not take action. Which of these is a good rule to remember at such a time?

answers.text: Leaving the road is always riskier than hitting another vehicle.
You can almost always turn to miss an obstacle more quickly than you can stop.
answers.explanation: In an emergency situation, it is often more expedient to turn to avoid an accident than to attempt to stop in time. Often, it is not feasible to stop in the amount of time or within the distance given and if a safe path can be identified veering to one side to avoid the collision may be the best action to take.

answers.text: Stopping is the safest action in a traffic emergency.

explanation: In an emergency situation, it is often more expedient to turn to avoid an accident than to attempt to stop in time. Often, it is not feasible to stop in the amount of time or within the distance given and if a safe path can be identified veering to one side to avoid the collision may be the best action to take.

question: Which of these would NOT help if windshield is covered with ice?

answers.text: Windshield scraper.
Dust brush.
answers.explanation: Remove ice from the windshield and mirrors with a window scraper prior to beginning the trip. Snow may be removed with a brush.

answers.text: Windshield defroster.

explanation: Remove ice from the windshield and mirrors with a window scraper prior to beginning the trip. Snow may be removed with a brush.

question: When you are driving on a snow packed road, you should reduce your speed by:

answers.explanation: The recommendations for severe weather include reducing speed by one-third for rain, half for snow, and down to a crawl and stopping as soon as it is convenient for ice.

explanation: The recommendations for severe weather include reducing speed by one-third for rain, half for snow, and down to a crawl and stopping as soon as it is convenient for ice.

question: Which driving strategy will help to prevent skidding?

answers.text: Accelerate as quickly as possible when merging.
Take curves as fast as possible to conserve fuel.
Slow your speed as needed based on driving conditions.
answers.explanation: Regardless of the posted speed limit, weather, terrain, and traffic conditions need to determine the speed that you choose to safely travel.

explanation: Regardless of the posted speed limit, weather, terrain, and traffic conditions need to determine the speed that you choose to safely travel.

question: Why is it important to shift gears correctly?

answers.text: To keep control of the vehicle.
answers.explanation: The gears of a transmission allow you to maximize the power of your engine to safely transport the cargo. Shifting into the wrong gear will compromise your ability to control the vehicle.

answers.text: To keep the engine oil flowing.
To keep the engine warm.

explanation: The gears of a transmission allow you to maximize the power of your engine to safely transport the cargo. Shifting into the wrong gear will compromise your ability to control the vehicle.

question: How do you correct at a drive-wheel acceleration skid?

answers.text: Stop accelerating.
answers.explanation: When a skid is caused by drive-wheel acceleration the quickest correction is simply to let off the accelerator pedal to stop accelerating.

answers.text: Downshift.
Apply more power to the wheels.

explanation: When a skid is caused by drive-wheel acceleration the quickest correction is simply to let off the accelerator pedal to stop accelerating.

question: What is the best way to load cargo to ensure the lowest center of gravity?

answers.text: Stack the cargo on each of the axles.
Load the cargo equally through the length of the trailer.
answers.explanation: To ensure the lowest center of gravity, load the cargo equally throughout the length of the trailer. Avoid stacking up the cargo in one or two areas.

answers.text: Fill the front of the trailer first and then gradually begin to move back.

explanation: To ensure the lowest center of gravity, load the cargo equally throughout the length of the trailer. Avoid stacking up the cargo in one or two areas.

question: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) means the following?

answers.text: Maximum gvw specified by the manufacturer
Maximum gvw specified by manufacturer for a single vehicle.
The value specified by the manufacturer as the loaded weight of a single vehicle.
answers.explanation: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVW) refers to the total weight of a truck combined with a single load as determined by the vehicle manufacturer.

explanation: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVW) refers to the total weight of a truck combined with a single load as determined by the vehicle manufacturer.

question: Which is the best description of reaction distance?

answers.text: The distance traveled between seeing a hazard and pressing the brake pedal.
answers.explanation: Reaction distance is the distance traveled between your brain recognizing a hazard and taking action, such as pressing the brake pedal.

answers.text: The distance traveled between seeing a hazard and the brakes activating at the wheels.
The distance traveled between seeing a hazard and coming to a stop.

explanation: Reaction distance is the distance traveled between your brain recognizing a hazard and taking action, such as pressing the brake pedal.

question: Which of these statements about backing a heavy vehicle is true?

answers.text: It is safer to back toward the right side of the vehicle than toward the driver's side.
You should avoid backing whenever you can.
answers.explanation: Backing a commercial truck should be a last resort and only done when absolutely necessary, such as backing into a loading dock.

answers.text: Helpers should be out of the driver's sight and use only voice signals to communicate with the driver.

explanation: Backing a commercial truck should be a last resort and only done when absolutely necessary, such as backing into a loading dock.

question: When should you test your parking brakes?

answers.text: When backing.
While the vehicle is parked.
answers.explanation: Using the seven-step inspection method, test the parking brakes and service brakes prior to beginning a trip.

answers.text: While moving at a very low speed.

explanation: Using the seven-step inspection method, test the parking brakes and service brakes prior to beginning a trip.

question: Which of these statements about acceleration is true?

answers.text: Rough acceleration can cause mechanical damage.
answers.explanation: Rapid acceleration or braking puts undue stress on mechanical components, causing the weakest portion to eventually fail.

answers.text: You should feel a jerking motion if you accelerate your vehicle properly.
When traction is poor, more power should be applied to the accelerator.

explanation: Rapid acceleration or braking puts undue stress on mechanical components, causing the weakest portion to eventually fail.

question: At what length must lights or flags be used to warn following drivers of a load that projects past the rear body of a vehicle?

answers.text: Two or more feet.
Three or more feet.
Four or more feet.
answers.explanation: Any load that extends beyond the rear body of a vehicle is a projecting load. Projecting loads require identification with either lights or flags. It is acceptable to use a 12-inch-square fluorescent orange or red flag during daytime hours. At night, you must use two red lights visible for at least 500 feet.

explanation: Any load that extends beyond the rear body of a vehicle is a projecting load. Projecting loads require identification with either lights or flags. It is acceptable to use a 12-inch-square fluorescent orange or red flag during daytime hours. At night, you must use two red lights visible for at least 500 feet.

question: Which of these statements about cargo loading is true?

answers.text: A state law dictates legal weight limit.
answers.explanation: Each state regulates its own weight limits for GVWR, GCWR, and axles. Axle weights are often tied to bridge limits. Be aware of the weight limits for any state where you are transporting.

answers.text: The legal maximum weight allowed by the state is considered safe for all driving conditions.
If cargo is loaded by the sipper a driver is not responsible for its load.

explanation: Each state regulates its own weight limits for GVWR, GCWR, and axles. Axle weights are often tied to bridge limits. Be aware of the weight limits for any state where you are transporting.

question: What areas should be monitored with your mirrors while driving?

answers.text: Cargo securement and anything abnormal with the tires.
answers.explanation: Mirrors should be adjusted to allow you to monitor your tires and cargo. When making a turn, especially at an intersection, monitor the position of the rear of the trailer to ensure that it clears any obstacles.

answers.text: The position of the front of the trailer during a turn.
Any vehicles or obstacles in your blind spots.

explanation: Mirrors should be adjusted to allow you to monitor your tires and cargo. When making a turn, especially at an intersection, monitor the position of the rear of the trailer to ensure that it clears any obstacles.

question: Which method of communication can tell following drivers that there is a hazard up ahead or that you intend to slow or stop?

answers.text: Activating your four-way flashers as you begin to slow down.
answers.explanation: Activating the four-way flashers informs following drivers that a hazard lay ahead. Tapping the brake pedal before beginning engine braking communicates your intention to slow down or stop.

answers.text: Turn on your signal to change lanes.
Begin downshifting to use engine braking.

explanation: Activating the four-way flashers informs following drivers that a hazard lay ahead. Tapping the brake pedal before beginning engine braking communicates your intention to slow down or stop.

question: Tourists may be hazardous because?

answers.text: Police do not fine them.
They may move slowly, unexpectedly change lanes or stop.
They drive rented cars.
answers.explanation: Tourists tend to be in areas where they are unfamiliar with the surroundings. They drive rented vehicles that they are also unfamiliar with. They are also distracted by looking at attractions or trying to navigate.

explanation: Tourists tend to be in areas where they are unfamiliar with the surroundings. They drive rented vehicles that they are also unfamiliar with. They are also distracted by looking at attractions or trying to navigate.

question: During the seven-step inspection method, which checks apply to wheel bearing seals?

answers.text: That the seals are not leaking grease.
answers.explanation: The wheel bearings themselves are not checked during the seven-step inspection. Ensure that the bearing seals are not leaking any grease.

answers.text: That the bearings are packed with grease.
That the correct type of lubricant has been used on the bearings.

explanation: The wheel bearings themselves are not checked during the seven-step inspection. Ensure that the bearing seals are not leaking any grease.

question: Most skids:

answers.text: Are caused by going too fast or trying to stop too quickly.
answers.explanation: Accelerating too quickly tends to cause drive wheels to skid. Attempting to stop too fast tends to cause non-drive wheels to skid.

answers.text: Do not happen with radial tires.
Happen on ice.

explanation: Accelerating too quickly tends to cause drive wheels to skid. Attempting to stop too fast tends to cause non-drive wheels to skid.

question: Which factor(s) will contribute to your choice of gear selection on a downgrade?

answers.text: The number of axles on the trailer.
The number of transmission gears.
The steepness of the grade.
answers.explanation: Multiple factors will come into play when selecting the right gear to use on a downgrade. Factors include the steepness of the grade, the weight of the load, weather conditions, road conditions, the presence of a retarder on the vehicle, the length of the grade, and possibly the condition of your tires and brakes.

explanation: Multiple factors will come into play when selecting the right gear to use on a downgrade. Factors include the steepness of the grade, the weight of the load, weather conditions, road conditions, the presence of a retarder on the vehicle, the length of the grade, and possibly the condition of your tires and brakes.

question: Brake fade:

answers.text: Can be caused by the brakes getting very hot.
answers.explanation: A certain amount of heat is generated during normal braking as kinetic energy is converted to thermal energy. However, if excessive heat builds up you may experience brake fade and eventually brake failure.

answers.text: Is a problem that only occurs with drum brakes.
Can be corrected by letting up on the brakes for 1-2 seconds and then reapplying them.

explanation: A certain amount of heat is generated during normal braking as kinetic energy is converted to thermal energy. However, if excessive heat builds up you may experience brake fade and eventually brake failure.

question: The purpose of the retarders is to:

answers.text: Apply extra braking power to the non-drive axles.
Help slow the vehicle while driving and reduce brake wear.
answers.explanation: Brake retarders may be of four types; engine, exhaust, hydraulic, or electrical. They are designed to reduce the speed of a vehicle similar to engine braking. This prolongs the life of the service brake components that are applied when the brake pedal is depressed.

answers.text: Help prevent skids.

explanation: Brake retarders may be of four types; engine, exhaust, hydraulic, or electrical. They are designed to reduce the speed of a vehicle similar to engine braking. This prolongs the life of the service brake components that are applied when the brake pedal is depressed.

question: What is the difference between cargo blocking and cargo bracing?

answers.text: Blocking runs from the upper portion of the cargo to the sides or floor of the cargo area.
Bracing can be placed on all sides of the cargo to prevent it from shifting in a probable direction of movement.
Blocking places materials in the way of the movement whereas bracing anchors the cargo from moving.
answers.explanation: Use blocking and/or bracing to help keep cargo secure from shifting and moving. Blocking places materials in the way of cargo movement. Bracing secures the cargo and anchors it to the sides or floor of the cargo area.

explanation: Use blocking and/or bracing to help keep cargo secure from shifting and moving. Blocking places materials in the way of cargo movement. Bracing secures the cargo and anchors it to the sides or floor of the cargo area.

question: Why is damage to the exhaust system a danger?

answers.text: Noise can damage the driver's ears.
Poisonous fumes can enter the cab or sleeping compartment.
answers.explanation: Damage to the exhaust system may cause carbon monoxide gas to enter the cab or sleeper posing a danger to the driver.

answers.text: You can pollute the air with the exhaust smoke.

explanation: Damage to the exhaust system may cause carbon monoxide gas to enter the cab or sleeper posing a danger to the driver.

question: Which of the statements about pre-trip inspection is true?

answers.text: When you park on a street, you should walk so you face away from an oncoming traffic.
Leave key in the ignition so you do not lose it when you are under the truck.
If you need to tilt a cab, secure loose things so that they could not fall or break anything.
answers.explanation: Some tractors will require that the cab be tilted to inspect the engine compartment. Be sure to secure anything in the cab that may move, fall, or shift when the cab is tilted.

explanation: Some tractors will require that the cab be tilted to inspect the engine compartment. Be sure to secure anything in the cab that may move, fall, or shift when the cab is tilted.

question: On a two-way undivided highway, level, where should you place reflective warning devices if you park along the right shoulder?

answers.text: One 10 feet behind the vehicle, a second 100 feet behind the vehicle, and the third 200 feet behind the vehicle.
One 100 feet to the front of the vehicle, a second 10 feet behind the vehicle, and the third 100 feet behind the vehicle.
answers.explanation: With a two-way undivided highway, one reflector will be 100 feet ahead of the vehicle. Two other reflectors will be 10 feet and 100 feet behind the vehicle.

answers.text: One 10 feet behind the vehicle, a second 100 feet behind the vehicle, and the third 500 feet behind the vehicle.

explanation: With a two-way undivided highway, one reflector will be 100 feet ahead of the vehicle. Two other reflectors will be 10 feet and 100 feet behind the vehicle.

question: A layer of ice on the road thin enough to view the road under it is called:

answers.text: Glare ice
Gray ice
Black ice
answers.explanation: Black ice is deceiving because it will look like a normal or wet roadway until you are right on top of it and realize that it is ice.

explanation: Black ice is deceiving because it will look like a normal or wet roadway until you are right on top of it and realize that it is ice.

question: To help you to stay alert while driving, you should:

answers.text: Take cold medicine if you have a cold.
Schedule trips for hours that you are normally asleep.
Take periodic breaks
answers.explanation: It is not worth the risk to attempt to drive when you are tired. Stop and get some rest whether it is a full night's sleep or just a daytime nap to refresh yourself.

explanation: It is not worth the risk to attempt to drive when you are tired. Stop and get some rest whether it is a full night's sleep or just a daytime nap to refresh yourself.

question: When testing your service brakes, what speed should you accelerate to prior to pressing the brakes?

answers.text: 5 mph
answers.explanation: Testing of the service brakes involves accelerating to 5 mph and then firmly depressing the brake. The truck should stop quickly. If the vehicle pulls to one side, then the brake toward the direction of the pull is operating correctly, but the brake in the opposite (non-pulling) direction should be checked.

answers.text: 10 mph
Do not accelerate. Firmly apply the brake, and then press the accelerator to see if you can move.

explanation: Testing of the service brakes involves accelerating to 5 mph and then firmly depressing the brake. The truck should stop quickly. If the vehicle pulls to one side, then the brake toward the direction of the pull is operating correctly, but the brake in the opposite (non-pulling) direction should be checked.

question: What strategy is recommended to mitigate wet brake issues?

answers.text: Drive with the parking brake applied.
Gently apply the brakes while moving slow and in a lower gear.
answers.explanation: This strategy is intended for driving through standing water. Shift to a low gear and proceed slowly while gently applying the brakes.

answers.text: Apply the brakes quick and firm once per minute.

explanation: This strategy is intended for driving through standing water. Shift to a low gear and proceed slowly while gently applying the brakes.

question: What should you do if you are unsure whether you have enough overhead clearance?

answers.text: Estimate the height of topped overhead object if it is not posted.
Find another route that will not require driving under the object.
answers.explanation: Always know the height of your load before starting a trip. Low underpasses for railroads and automotive bridges are nearly always marked with the clearance they allow. If you do not know for sure that you can clear the obstacle, do not take any chances and locate a parallel route to bypass the obstacle.

answers.text: Slow down slightly and try to drive under object.

explanation: Always know the height of your load before starting a trip. Low underpasses for railroads and automotive bridges are nearly always marked with the clearance they allow. If you do not know for sure that you can clear the obstacle, do not take any chances and locate a parallel route to bypass the obstacle.

question: Federal regulations mandate that drivers carry which emergency related item?

answers.text: Flare gun
Roadside reflectors
answers.explanation: Federal regulations mandate that a driver always carry a fire extinguisher and roadside warning devices consisting of three reflective triangles, three liquid flares, and six fuses.

answers.text: First aid kit

explanation: Federal regulations mandate that a driver always carry a fire extinguisher and roadside warning devices consisting of three reflective triangles, three liquid flares, and six fuses.

question: What keeps an engine cool in hot weather driving?

answers.text: Coolant in the engine cooling system
answers.explanation: The primary method of cooling an engine is the cooling system. Coolant helps the engine under hot conditions as well as cold conditions

answers.text: High speed driving in order to put more air to the radiator.
Air conditioner use.

explanation: The primary method of cooling an engine is the cooling system. Coolant helps the engine under hot conditions as well as cold conditions

question: The center of gravity of a load:

answers.text: Should be kept as high as possible.
Can make a vehicle more likely to tip over on curves if it is high.
answers.explanation: A vehicle's center of gravity is correlated with its tendency to roll over. A higher center of gravity increases the chance of a rollover. Try to load a trailer in a way that results in the lowest center of gravity.

answers.text: Is only a problem if the vehicle is overloaded.

explanation: A vehicle's center of gravity is correlated with its tendency to roll over. A higher center of gravity increases the chance of a rollover. Try to load a trailer in a way that results in the lowest center of gravity.

question: If your vehicle catches fire while you are driving, you should:

answers.text: Increase your speed to put out the flames.
Park where a building or frees shelter your vehicle from the wind.
Park in an open area.
answers.explanation: Ideally, park in an area that is isolated from anything else that may catch fire such as buildings, gas pumps, vegetation, or other vehicles.

explanation: Ideally, park in an area that is isolated from anything else that may catch fire such as buildings, gas pumps, vegetation, or other vehicles.

question: When are helpers needed to back your vehicle?

answers.text: Only if you have a trailer on your vehicle.
Only if you need to park.
Whenever you have to back.
answers.explanation: Ideally, an assistant will help monitor your blind spots whenever you back a commercial vehicle.

explanation: Ideally, an assistant will help monitor your blind spots whenever you back a commercial vehicle.

question: If you are not sure what to use to put out a hazardous material fire you should:

answers.text: Use dirt.
Wait for fire fighters.
answers.explanation: Some extinguisher media may actually make a fire worse when applied. If you do not know what type of media to apply to a fire, just wait for professional firefighters.

answers.text: Use water.

explanation: Some extinguisher media may actually make a fire worse when applied. If you do not know what type of media to apply to a fire, just wait for professional firefighters.

question: Regardless of cargo size, every cargo needs to be secured with a minimum of:

answers.text: 4 tie-downs
2 tie-downs
answers.explanation: Regardless of cargo size, a minimum of two tie-downs must be used to secure every cargo.

answers.text: 3 tie-downs

explanation: Regardless of cargo size, a minimum of two tie-downs must be used to secure every cargo.

question: What three distances add up the total stopping distance of your truck or bus?

answers.text: Attention distance, reaction distance, and slowing distance.
Perception distance, reaction distance, and braking distance.
answers.explanation: Total distance is equal to the sum of perception distance plus reaction distance, plus braking distance.

answers.text: Respond distance, observation distance, and braking distance.

explanation: Total distance is equal to the sum of perception distance plus reaction distance, plus braking distance.

question: Monitoring the roadway for potential hazards includes:

answers.text: Viewing the roadway 12 to 15 seconds ahead
answers.explanation: Ensure that mirrors are properly adjusted before starting a trip. Continuously monitor the area 12 to 15 seconds ahead as well as traffic to the sides and rear using your mirrors.

answers.text: Using mirrors to monitor blind spots
Keeping both hands on the steering wheel

explanation: Ensure that mirrors are properly adjusted before starting a trip. Continuously monitor the area 12 to 15 seconds ahead as well as traffic to the sides and rear using your mirrors.

question: Which of these statements about engine overheating is true?

answers.text: You should never remove the radiator cap on a pressurized system until the system is cooled.
answers.explanation: As the engine coolant mixture heats it will expand and build up pressure. Removing the radiator cap allows this pressure to vent to the atmosphere, very possibly causing severe burns as the steam and boiling coolant spray you. Never remove a radiator cap until the engine has cooled down.

answers.text: Antifreeze is not needed when the weather is warm.
You should never shut off an overheated engine until it cools.

explanation: As the engine coolant mixture heats it will expand and build up pressure. Removing the radiator cap allows this pressure to vent to the atmosphere, very possibly causing severe burns as the steam and boiling coolant spray you. Never remove a radiator cap until the engine has cooled down.

question: During step 3 of the seven-step inspection method, in which gear should a manual transmission be placed once you get into the cab?

answers.text: Park
First
Neutral
answers.explanation: Step 3 involves setting the parking brake, starting the engine to listen for abnormal noises, and ensuring that a manual transmission is in neutral or an automatic transmission is in park.

explanation: Step 3 involves setting the parking brake, starting the engine to listen for abnormal noises, and ensuring that a manual transmission is in neutral or an automatic transmission is in park.

question: How many alcoholic drinks per hour can the liver metabolize to eliminate the effects of the alcohol?

answers.explanation: The liver metabolizes alcohol about one alcoholic drink per hour. One drink is approximately a 12-ounce beer, one shot, or half a glass of wine.

explanation: The liver metabolizes alcohol about one alcoholic drink per hour. One drink is approximately a 12-ounce beer, one shot, or half a glass of wine.

question: Which may be a sign of tire failure?

answers.text: A loud hissing noise.
Vibration.
answers.explanation: Vibrations can be a major signal that a tire has failed. Failures of the front tires will be felt in the steering wheel. Failures of the other wheels will be felt through your seat. However, tires that are the furthest back on the trailer will be the hardest to feel.

answers.text: Wheels skidding.

explanation: Vibrations can be a major signal that a tire has failed. Failures of the front tires will be felt in the steering wheel. Failures of the other wheels will be felt through your seat. However, tires that are the furthest back on the trailer will be the hardest to feel.

question: Which condition would indicate that the hydraulic brake system requires service?

answers.text: There is brake pull to either the left or right during testing.
answers.explanation: The hydraulic brakes will require service if the brake pedal fades or goes to the floor within five seconds of pumping it up. They will also need service if there is a brake pull to the left or right when pressing the brakes while driving at a slow speed.

answers.text: The brake pedal is firm for longer than 5 seconds during the test.
A half-inch long hair-line crack on a brake drum.

explanation: The hydraulic brakes will require service if the brake pedal fades or goes to the floor within five seconds of pumping it up. They will also need service if there is a brake pull to the left or right when pressing the brakes while driving at a slow speed.

question: Which condition may result from excessive water on the brake pads/shoes?

answers.text: Pad/shoe cracking
Increased brake lining wear
Increased stopping distance
answers.explanation: Excessive water on the brake pads/shoes can result in less friction against the rotor/drum, causing an increase in stopping distance.

explanation: Excessive water on the brake pads/shoes can result in less friction against the rotor/drum, causing an increase in stopping distance.

question: Which indicator can be used to time proper upshifts and downshifts?

answers.text: Engine RPM
answers.explanation: Engine RPM and vehicle speed can let a driver know when they should upshift when accelerating. or downshift for engine braking. Ideally, the best engine RPM range will be when the engine is producing the best torque.

answers.text: Engine oil pressure
Engine oil temperature

explanation: Engine RPM and vehicle speed can let a driver know when they should upshift when accelerating. or downshift for engine braking. Ideally, the best engine RPM range will be when the engine is producing the best torque.

question: The value specified by the manufacturer as the loaded weight of a combination vehicle is?

answers.text: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating
Gross Combination Weight
Gross Combination Weight Rating
answers.explanation: Cross Combination Weight Rating (GCWR) refers to the total combined weight of a truck combined with its articulated load, as determined by the vehicle manufacturer.

explanation: Cross Combination Weight Rating (GCWR) refers to the total combined weight of a truck combined with its articulated load, as determined by the vehicle manufacturer.

question: The seven-step inspection method includes the following, EXCEPT:

answers.text: 3. Start the engine and inspect inside the cab.
6. Check signal lights.
7. Complete and submit the vehicle service report.
answers.explanation: The steps of the seven-step inspection method include: Vehicle Overview, Check Engine Compartment, Start Engine and Inspect Inside Cab, Turn-off Engine and Check Lights, Do Walk Around Inspection, Check Signal Lights, and Start Engine and Check.

explanation: The steps of the seven-step inspection method include: Vehicle Overview, Check Engine Compartment, Start Engine and Inspect Inside Cab, Turn-off Engine and Check Lights, Do Walk Around Inspection, Check Signal Lights, and Start Engine and Check.

question: When you are passing a vehicle, pedestrian, or bicycle you should assume that:

answers.text: They may be moving to your traffic lane.
answers.explanation: When passing a vehicle, pedestrian, or bicycle, never assume that they can see you. They may pull into your lane at any moment. Be prepared to stop quickly or take evasive action.

answers.text: They see your vehicle.
They know you are to pass.

explanation: When passing a vehicle, pedestrian, or bicycle, never assume that they can see you. They may pull into your lane at any moment. Be prepared to stop quickly or take evasive action.

question: Which statement is true regarding CMV accidents?

answers.text: Most CMV accidents are caused by other motorists.
Most CMV accidents occur due to equipment failure.
Most CMV accidents occur when drivers are tired.
answers.explanation: Most CMV accidents are caused by drivers who are tired and are no longer alert, especially between midnight and 6am.

explanation: Most CMV accidents are caused by drivers who are tired and are no longer alert, especially between midnight and 6am.
